full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding the efficient relaxation and how to implement Eq. 12 with sklearn SVC
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: In the iSVM paper (Section 3.1), the efficient relaxation leads to T cost-sensitive SVMs (Eq. 12) with per-sample weights phi_d^t. How can I implement this using sklearn.svm.SVC with sample_weight parameter?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed that sklearn SVC sample_weight scales the C parameter per sample, which is equivalent to cost-sensitive SVM in Eq.(12)
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified by checking sklearn SVC documentation: sample_weight multiplies C for each sample, producing the weighted hinge loss in Eq.(12)

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Implementing the phi update (Eq. 11) with stick-breaking expectations
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: How do I compute E[log v_t] and E[log(1-v_t)] for a Beta distribution Beta(nu1, nu2) in the variational DP inference? What is the closed form?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided the closed form: E[log v_t] = psi(nu1) - psi(nu1+nu2) and E[log(1-v_t)] = psi(nu2) - psi(nu1+nu2) using the digamma function
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified against Blei & Jordan (2006) cited in the paper and confirmed using scipy.special.digamma in my implementation
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: I adapted the code to use scipy.special.digamma and integrated it into the _e_log_v and _e_log_1_minus_v methods of the iSVM class.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Debugging numerical stability in the softmax phi update
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: My phi update using exp(log_phi) is producing NaN values when some log_phi entries are very negative. How can I stabilise this numerically?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Suggested the log-sum-exp trick: subtract log_phi.max(axis=1, keepdims=True) before exponentiating
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Standard numerical trick; confirmed it eliminates NaN while preserving the normalised probabilities
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: I applied the log-sum-exp shift inside the fit() method's phi update loop, after computing all T log_phi columns.

top_5_prompts.prompt: How do I compute E[log v_t] and E[log(1-v_t)] for a Beta distribution Beta(nu1, nu2) in the variational DP inference? What is the closed form?
top_5_prompts.why_important: This was the most mathematically critical implementation detail — getting the digamma expectations wrong would break the entire DP-VI update in _log_stick_weights().

top_5_prompts.prompt: My phi update using exp(log_phi) is producing NaN values when some log_phi entries are very negative. How can I stabilise this numerically?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Without numerical stability the training loop failed completely. The log-sum-exp trick is standard but I needed confirmation on where to apply it in the iSVM context.

top_5_prompts.prompt: In the iSVM paper (Section 3.1), the efficient relaxation leads to T cost-sensitive SVMs (Eq. 12) with per-sample weights phi_d^t. How can I implement this using sklearn.svm.SVC with sample_weight parameter?
top_5_prompts.why_important: This clarified the key implementation shortcut that makes the paper's algorithm practical using standard libraries.
